Albay Province, Bicol Region

Living in the
shadow of
compound risk

Albay sits at the intersection of two of the Philippines' most severe hazard systems: Mayon Volcano, the most active in the country, and the Pacific typhoon corridor, which delivers 5–7 destructive storms per decade. This is not simply a physical risk problem. The communities most exposed are also those carrying the deepest social vulnerability — informal land tenure, limited documentation, economic fragility — that turns an eruption into a years-long displacement.

This tool maps where hazards and vulnerability overlap — and where the formal aid system fails the communities that need it most.

Albay SVHI
Compound risk

Albay sits at the intersection of volcanic, typhoon, and flood hazard — and the communities most exposed are the same ones carrying the deepest social vulnerability. Toggle physical and social layers together to see where risk truly compounds.

Key communities to explore▼

Key Communities

The invisible barrier

Physical exposure determines who is in danger. Land tenure determines who gets help. An estimated 40–60% of households in PDZ municipalities hold land informally — locked out of the formal aid system when disaster strikes.

~72k
Est. informal households
province-wide
40–60%
Informal tenure rate
in PDZ municipalities
~72k estimate: Derived from PSA 2020 CPH household counts per municipality, weighted by tenure insecurity proxies (% rent-free without consent + field-estimated informal rates for PDZ zones). Not a direct census count — the PSA "rent-free without consent" category is a known undercount of actual informal tenure.

40–60%: Estimated range for PDZ-adjacent municipalities based on DILG field assessments and IOM HLP programme intake. Formal PSA 2020 housing tenure tables for Albay are not publicly accessible at time of publication.
System failure analysis

Eruptions don't just displace people — they trigger cascading failures across road, power, health, water, and aid access systems simultaneously. Use the simulator in the panel to trace how failures compound.

Here's how to read this panel
Each section below is expandable. Start at the top — the story builds as you scroll.
1
The Gap opens first
Why standard risk models undercount this place — physical hazard score vs. the full picture including social vulnerability.
2
Vulnerability Breakdown
Bar charts showing every physical and social dimension side by side — so you can see exactly what's elevated.
3
What's Driving It
Sensitivity, exposure, and adaptive capacity unpacked — the three root dimensions behind the composite score.
4
Actions & Guidance
What can be done — phase by phase: Preparedness, Response, Recovery, Mitigation, and Adaptation.
5
Gaps & Needs
Current priority gaps to address before the next event — tenure, routes, communications, site audits.

NHA Sites
Three overlapping systems shape Albay's risk profile: volcanic (PDZ/EDZ rings, lahar channels), typhoon (track corridors, storm surge), and compound (lahar-flood overlap during typhoon season when Mayon is active). Vulnerability layers add social exposure on top of physical hazard.

Data Sources

All layers are based on publicly available or IOM-cited sources. Scores are modeled estimates for demonstration.

🌋 Volcanic Hazard
PHIVOLCS Mayon Volcano Alert System
0–5 alert scale; PDZ (6km), EDZ (7km) boundaries. APSEMO pre-emptive expansion to 8km documented in SP Resolution 155-94 (1994).
Applied Volcanology of Mayon (PMC)
Lahar channel mapping (Miisi, Bonga, Buyuan, Padang gullies). PDC flow simulations.
🌀 Typhoon Hazard
PAGASA Typhoon Track Archive
Reming/Durian (2006), Nina/Nock-Ten (2016), Queenie/Quinta (2020). IBTrACS historical best-track data.
🏘️ Resettlement / Tenure
PMC Study: NHA Resettlement Risk (PMC7989691)
25 NHA sites, z-score compound risk. Oson (4.54), Miisi (4.19), Pinabobong (3.40), Cullat (3.32) as top-4 at-risk.
APSEMO Zero-Casualty Strategy (Naz et al., Bicol Univ.)
Six-strategy framework documentation. SP Resolution 155-94 citation.
📊 Social Vulnerability
PSA 2020 Census / CBMS
Municipality-level economic fragility indicators, population, housing typology data. Albay Province.
🔑 IOM / LandLedger
IOM Philippines HLP Programming
LandLedger deployment in Albay. Tenure documentation for PDZ and resettlement communities. Program data cited from IOM briefing materials.
